description_long: This is a pair of metal pliers with a robust, utilitarian design, commonly used for gripping, bending, and cutting wires or other materials. The handles are slightly curved and appear to be made entirely of metal, lacking any visible insulation or ergonomic padding. The jaws feature a combination of flat and notched gripping surfaces, with a central circular notch likely intended for gripping round objects such as pipes or bolts. The pivot mechanism is a classic fixed joint, allowing the jaws to open and close smoothly. The surface of the pliers is dark, with visible wear and a matte, slightly rough finish, suggesting frequent use. No visible branding or logo is present on the tool.
description: A sturdy, all-metal pair of pliers with curved handles and notched jaws, showing signs of wear and a matte finish. The tool is utilitarian and lacks any visible branding or insulation.
description_view_0: Side profile showing the thin, flat alignment of the jaws and handles, with the pivot joint visible as a slight bulge.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the open, notched jaws and the full curvature of the handles, highlighting the gripping and cutting surfaces.
description_view_2: Opposite side profile, similar to view 0, emphasizing the flatness and thickness of the tool.
description_view_3: Rear view of the pliers, showing the back of the jaws and the handles' curvature, with the pivot joint centered.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, uninsulated metal handles with a basic cylindrical profile.
attribute_jaw_design: Flat and notched jaws with a central circular notch for gripping round objects; no serrations or needle-nose features.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed single pivot joint, allowing standard opening and closing motion without adjustable width.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal with no visible ribbing, knurling, or additional grip texture.
